summary: PEB-Sim platform — a 3D Pre-Engineered Building simulation tool. Phase 3 adds: Auto-Drafting Engine for 2D GA drawings (SVG/PDF), LOD 350 high-fidelity connection detail modals with interactive hotspots, a Collaborative Smart Share system (public read-only URLs, 3D sticky-note annotations, real-time sync via Supabase Realtime), a Commercial Costing Engine with rate card inputs and dynamic quoting, and advanced export options (STAAD.Pro .std / IFC BIM formats).
architectural_notes: DrawingGenerator service converts 3D model state to 2D vector views (SVG/PDF) using react-pdf or server-side matplotlib
LOD 350 detail modals triggered by clickable hotspot nodes placed at key structural joints in the 3D scene
Smart Share uses hashed URLs for public read-only mode; real-time collaboration via Supabase Realtime/WebSockets
Database schema requires new tables: Comments and SharedLinks
CostEstimator function reacts to building dimension changes and rate card inputs to update total cost in real-time

existing_features.title: Implement a 2D GA Drawing Generator that takes the current 3D building model state (dimensions, bay spacing, roof pitch, purlin/girt positions, bracing layout, anchor bolt locations) and renders four standardized 2D SVG views: (1) Anchor Bolt Plan showing pedestal locations and column reactions, (2) Cross Section showing clear height and width with member labels, (3) Roof Plan showing purlin spacing and bracing locations, (4) Side Elevation showing bay spacing and wall bracing. Package all four views into a downloadable PDF file titled 'General Arrangement (GA) Drawings'. Use a client-side SVG-to-PDF approach (e.g., jsPDF + SVG rendering) since server-side Python/matplotlib is unavailable. Add a 'Download GA Drawings' button in the engineering panel or export section of the ProjectViewer.

Implement LOD 350 high-fidelity connection detail hotspots on the 3D model. Place clickable sphere 'node' markers at key structural joints: Rafter-to-Column Haunch (both ends of each rafter), Ridge Connection (apex), and Base Plate (bottom of each column). When a node is clicked, open a modal dialog showing a high-resolution Three.js or SVG detail view of that connection type. Base Plate detail must show: anchor bolt layout (4-bolt pattern), stiffener plates, and a grout gap between plate and concrete. Haunch detail must show: flange plates, bolt rows (2×4 grid), and web stiffeners. Also render Concrete Pedestals (grey box geometry) and Foundation Blocks (larger grey box below grade, partially clipped) beneath each column base in the main 3D scene to visualize the steel-to-civil interface.

Implement the frontend Public Share view. Add a React route at /share/:token that loads the project via getProjectByShareToken. This view renders the full BuildingSimulator in read-only mode: all editing controls (side-panel inputs, branding controls, engineering inputs, etc.) are hidden or disabled. Show a 'View Only' badge in the header. Add a 'Share' button in the ProjectViewer (authenticated view) that calls createSharedLink and displays the generated share URL in a copyable dialog.

Implement the 3D Annotation (Sticky Note) UI in ProjectViewer. When the authenticated user double-clicks on a structural element (column, rafter, etc.) in the 3D scene, show a small input popover anchored near the click point to type a comment, then submit it via addComment. Render all existing comments for the project as floating 3D billboard markers (e.g., a yellow sticky-note icon + author initial) positioned at their stored world-space coordinates. Add a collapsible CommentSidebar panel listing all annotations with element reference, author, text, and a delete button (for own comments). The share/read-only view shows markers and sidebar in read-only mode (no add/delete).

Implement the Commercial Costing Engine. Add a 'Rate Card' settings panel (accessible via a tab or modal in ProjectViewer) with four numeric inputs: Primary Steel ($/MT), Secondary Steel ($/MT), Sheeting ($/sqm), and Erection Labor ($/sqm). Persist these rates in local component state (no backend persistence required). Add a `calculateProjectCost` utility function that multiplies each rate against the corresponding calculated quantities from `calculateBuildingStats` (primary steel weight in MT, secondary steel weight in MT, total sheeting area in sqm, floor area in sqm for labor). Display the computed 'Total Project Cost' as a formatted currency value in the engineering sidebar panel, updating reactively whenever building dimensions or rate card values change.

Implement Advanced Export options for STAAD.Pro (.std) and IFC (BIM) formats. Add an 'Export' section in ProjectViewer (e.g., in the engineering panel or a dedicated Export tab) with two buttons: 'Export STAAD.Pro (.std)' and 'Export IFC (.ifc)'. The STAAD.Pro export generates a plain-text .std file encoding the center-line node coordinates and member connectivity (JOINT COORDINATES and MEMBER INCIDENCES sections) from the current building geometry. The IFC export generates a minimal IFC 2x3 text file encoding IfcColumn and IfcBeam entities with their center-line geometry. Both exports trigger a browser file download.

new_feature_requests.summary: Auto-Drafting Engine: generate 2D GA drawings (Anchor Bolt Plan, Cross Section, Roof Plan, Side Elevation) as SVG/PDF downloadable package
LOD 350 connection detail modals: interactive hotspots at structural joints (haunch, ridge, base plate) showing high-res detail including concrete pedestals/foundation blocks
Collaborative Smart Share: unique hashed read-only URLs, 3D sticky-note annotations on elements, real-time model sync via WebSockets/Supabase Realtime
Commercial Costing Engine: rate card settings panel (Primary Steel, Secondary Steel, Sheeting, Erection Labor) with real-time dynamic total project cost in sidebar
Advanced export: center-line geometry export to STAAD.Pro .std and IFC BIM formats for structural analysis

Implement a backend data model for SharedLinks. Each SharedLink record must store: a unique hashed token (e.g., 8-char alphanumeric), the source projectId, the ownerPrincipal, and a createdAt timestamp. Add backend endpoints: createSharedLink(projectId) → returns the token, getProjectByShareToken(token) → returns project data in read-only form (no mutation access). Store SharedLinks in stable backend state.

Implement a backend data model for Comments (3D Annotations). Each Comment record must store: a unique ID, the projectId it belongs to, the author principal, the target element identifier (e.g., 'column-2'), the 3D world-space position (x, y, z floats), the comment text, and a createdAt timestamp. Add backend endpoints: addComment(projectId, elementId, position, text) → returns the created Comment, getComments(projectId) → returns all comments for a project, deleteComment(commentId) → removes a comment (owner or project-owner only).
